chunk:
CHUNK:016/045
Conclusion
In this study, we investigate a recently proposed task,
Temporal Sentence Grounding with Glance Annotation. Under this setting, we propose a Dynamic Gaussian
prior based Grounding framework with Glance annota-
tion, termed D3G. Specifically, D3G consists of a Semantic
Alignment Group Contrastive Learning module (SA-GCL)
and a Dynamic Gaussian prior Adjustment module (DGA). SA-GCL aims to mine a wide range of positive moments
and align the positive sentence-moment pairs in the joint
embedding space. DGA effectively alleviates the annota-
tion bias and models complex query consisting of multiple
events via dynamically adjusting the Gaussian prior with
multiple Gaussian functions, promoting the precision of lo-
calization. Extensive experiments show that D3G signifi-
cantly narrows the performance gap between fully super-
vised methods and glance supervised methods. Without ex-
cessive interaction of visual-language, D3G provides a con-
cise framework and a fresh insight to the challenging tem-
poral sentence grounding under low-cost glance annotation. Limitations. Although D3G achieves promising improve-
ments with glance annotations, it still has some limitations. In this paper, the DAG adjusts Gaussian prior via the com-
bination of multiple fixed scale Gaussian functions. It fails
to scale down the Gaussian distribution to fit the small mo-
ments. It is expected to explore dynamic learnable Gaussian
functions to model moment of arbitrary duration in future
work. Besides, the sampling strategy for SA-GCL is still
not enough flexible to sample accurate positive moments. References

compressed_context:
H2: CONCLUSION  
METHOD: D3G=DYNAMIC-GAUSSIAN-PRIOR-BASED-GROUNDING W/ GLANCE-ANNOTATION  
MODULES:  
  SA-GCL: SEMANTIC-ALIGNMENT-GROUP-CONTRASTIVE-LEARNING  
  DGA: DYNAMIC-GAUSSIAN-PRIOR-ADJUSTMENT  
SA-GCL: MINES WIDE-RANGE POSITIVE-MOMENTS ∧ ALIGN SENTENCE-MOMENT PAIRS IN JOINT EMBEDDING  
DGA: ALLEVIATES ANNOTATION-BIAS ∧ MODELS MULTI-EVENT QUERIES  
  VIA DYNAMIC GAUSSIAN-PRIOR COMPOSED OF MULTI GAUSSIANS  
RESULT: D3G NARROWS PERFORMANCE-GAP BETWEEN FULLY-SUPERVISED ∧ GLANCE-SUPERVISED METHODS  
ADVANTAGE: NO EXCESSIVE VISUAL-LANGUAGE INTERACTION; PROVIDES CONCISE FRAMEWORK ∧ FRESH INSIGHT FOR LOW-COST GLANCE ANNOTATION  

H2: LIMITATIONS  
DGA: USES FIXED-SCALE GAUSSIANS ∧ FAILS SCALE-DOWN FOR SHORT-MOMENTS  
FUTURE: EXPLORE DYNAMIC-LEARNABLE GAUSSIAN FUNCTIONS FOR ARBITRARY-DURATION MOMENTS  
SA-GCL: SAMPLING-STRATEGY NOT FLEXIBLE ENOUGH FOR ACCURATE POSITIVE-MOMENTS  

H2: REFERENCES  
REF1: HENDRICKS+17[ICCV]: LOCALIZING MOMENTS IN VIDEO W/ NATURAL LANGUAGE
